title: calathea regalis
intro: Calathea regalis is a species of flowering plant in the family Marantaceae, commonly known as a prayer plant due to its unique ability to fold its leaves upwards at night resembling praying hands. The plant is believed to have medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic compounds that exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Consuming Calathea regalis has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and potentially lowering blood sugar levels. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the Calathea regalis plant can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, although its use should be done under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to the lack of extensive research on its effects in humans.
